prompt_inputs.goal: Strength and power development for weightlifting competition
prompt_inputs.restrictions: Vegetarian, dislikes legumes and tofu, prefers simple whole foods

solution_criteria: Meal plan covers 3 meals within 1 day with adequate protein distribution (minimum 1.6g per kg bodyweight)
All meals comply with vegetarian restrictions and stated food preferences
Plan is compact and concise (suitable for quick reference)
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete
scenario: Testing with a strength/power athlete (weightlifter) with dietary restrictions (vegetarian) and limited food preferences, requiring protein optimization

prompt_inputs.goal: maintain energy and performance for a professional soccer match with minimal preparation time
prompt_inputs.restrictions: no nuts or peanuts due to allergy

solution_criteria: Includes exactly 3-4 meals/snacks appropriate for match day timing
All meals require minimal prep time (under 10 minutes or ready-to-eat)
Meals are budget-conscious and commonly available
Plan avoids nuts/peanuts and supports soccer performance demands
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete
scenario: Testing with a sport-specific athlete (professional soccer player) with realistic budget constraints and minimal meal prep time available during competition day

prompt_inputs.goal: Marathon training with carbohydrate loading and elevated caloric intake for endurance performance

solution_criteria: Meal plan spans 24 hours with 4-5 meals/snacks strategically timed around training sessions
Total daily calories support endurance athlete needs (approximately 3000-3500 kcal for 72kg athlete)
Carbohydrate-focused macronutrient distribution with emphasis on pre- and post-workout timing
Plan is concise and practical (fits in 1-2 paragraphs or simple table format)
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete
scenario: Testing with a high-performance endurance athlete (marathon runner) requiring elevated caloric intake and carbohydrate loading, with specific timing around training sessions
